milestone.description: explain why === is safer than == in JavaScript

milestone.description: explain what a LEFT JOIN returns

milestone.description: explain why the mean is more sensitive to outliers than the median

context.text: Nine houses cost about 300k and one costs 30 million. Why does that wreck the mean but barely touch the median?
the huge price gets added straight into the total so it drags the average way up, but the median only cares about the middle position so it basically ignores the freak value

output: {"achieved": true, "evidence": "Student explained the outlier \"gets added straight into the total\" while the median \"only cares about the middle position\" — the correct mechanism."}

milestone.description: explain where the released heat comes from in an exothermic reaction

output: {"achieved": true, "evidence": "Student correctly said forming new bonds \"gives off more energy than it cost to break the old ones\" and the surplus escapes as heat."}
